ingredients.name: Japan International Cooperation Agency (JICA) Yokohama Center
Asahi Holiday Emergency Clinic
ingredients.entityContext: The emergency medical clinic in Asahi ward that appears in episode 13's crucial hospital scene, where Sakuta faces the most dire consequences of the film's central adolescence syndrome crisis.

ingredients.name: Asahi Holiday Emergency Clinic
ingredients.entityContext: The emergency medical clinic in Asahi ward that appears in episode 13's crucial hospital scene, where Sakuta faces the most dire consequences of the film's central adolescence syndrome crisis.

ingredients.name: New Chitose Airport Onsen
ingredients.entityContext: The airport onsen facility at New Chitose Airport in Hokkaido, where a rest-stop scene occurs in episode 13 during the film's travel sequence to and from Hokkaido.

ingredients.name: New Chitose Airport Station
ingredients.entityContext: The underground station connecting New Chitose Airport to central Hokkaido, the departure and arrival point in episode 13's Hokkaido sequences.

ingredients.name: Haneda Airport
ingredients.entityContext: The main Tokyo-area airport used as the gateway for the film's long-distance travel scenes in episode 13, a real-world anchor for the emotional journeys characters take.

ingredients.name: Haneda Airport
ingredients.entityContext: The main Tokyo-area airport used as the gateway for the film's long-distance travel scenes in episode 13, a real-world anchor for the emotional journeys characters take.

ingredients.name: Harbor View Park Sunken Flower Bed
ingredients.localName: 沈床花壇 香りの庭
ingredients.entityContext: The sunken rose garden at Yokohama's Harbor View Park (Minato no Mieru Oka Koen), seen in episode 12 as Sakuta walks the hilltop park with a heavy heart ahead of the film's finale.

ingredients.name: Christmas Toys
ingredients.entityContext: The shop near Yokohama's Motomachi district that appears in episode 12's Christmas Eve sequence, its festive display setting the bittersweet seasonal atmosphere of the film's climax.

ingredients.name: Yokohama Foreign General Cemetery
ingredients.entityContext: The historic hillside cemetery in Yokohama's Yamate district, visible from the road in episode 12's Motomachi scenes, its Western gravestones adding a quietly melancholic tone.

ingredients.name: Foreign Quarter Border Stone
ingredients.localName: 道路境界石・居留地境界石

ingredients.name: Kanazawachiku Center Sports Park
ingredients.localName: 金沢地区センタースポーツ広場

ingredients.name: Kanazawa-Hakkei Station
ingredients.entityContext: The station near the Yokohama City University campus, used as a key transit point in episode 12 as the story's events near the university reach a breaking point.

ingredients.name: Fujisawa Civic Hall Concert
ingredients.entityContext: The concert hall in Fujisawa that hosts the Mai Sakurajima performance scene in episode 12, the on-stage moment carrying enormous emotional weight as Sakuta watches from the audience.

ingredients.name: Yamasen Bridge Ruins
ingredients.entityContext: The historic iron railway bridge ruins in Hokkaido that appear in episode 11, representing Shoko's northern hometown and the emotional distance Sakuta travels to reach her.

ingredients.name: Glass Pyramid "HIDAMARI"
ingredients.localName: ガラスのピラミッド HIDAMARI
ingredients.entityContext: The iconic glass pyramid at the Sapporo Art Park appears in episode 11 as the striking location where Sakuta confronts memories and decisions tied to Shoko's fate.

ingredients.name: Nicolai Bergmann Hakone Gardens
ingredients.localName: ニコライ バーグマン 箱根 ガーデンズ
ingredients.entityContext: The scenic floral garden in Hakone visited in episode 11, offering a lush backdrop for a reflective scene as Sakuta grapples with the film's central supernatural dilemma.

ingredients.name: Houkokuji
ingredients.entityContext: The bamboo grove temple in Kamakura visited by Sakuta and Shoko in episode 10, its serene garden providing a moment of calm before the film's emotional climax builds.

ingredients.name: Kamakura Mameya
ingredients.localName: 鎌倉まめや 小町通り店
ingredients.entityContext: The popular bean confectionery shop on Komachi-dori in Kamakura, passed by in episode 10 during the characters' Kamakura stroll, a recognisable landmark along the shrine approach.

ingredients.name: Kamakura Kanzashi Aki Studio
ingredients.localName: 鎌倉かんざし あき工房

ingredients.name: Kamakura Kimono Rental Waraku
ingredients.localName: 鎌倉着物レンタル和楽

ingredients.name: Kamakura Ichibanya
ingredients.localName: 鎌倉壱番屋 小町店

ingredients.name: Tsurugaoka Hachimangu Shrine 2nd Torii
ingredients.localName: 鶴岡八幡宮 二の鳥居
ingredients.entityContext: The second torii gate on Wakamiya-oji approach to Kamakura's great shrine, passed by characters in episode 10 during a Kamakura walk that deepens Sakuta's understanding of Shoko.

ingredients.name: Tully's Coffee Saikaya Fujisawa Store
ingredients.localName: タリーズコーヒー さいか屋藤沢店

ingredients.name: Kurieito S D Fujisawaekikitaguchiten
ingredients.localName: クリエイトＳ・Ｄ 藤沢駅北口店

ingredients.name: Gokurakuji Station
ingredients.entityContext: The tiny, flower-lined Enoden station near Kamakura that appears in episode 8 travel scenes, beloved by fans of the whole Bunny Girl Senpai franchise.

ingredients.name: Tiffany & Co. Yokohama Landmark Plaza Store
ingredients.localName: ティファニー 横浜ランドマークプラザ店
ingredients.entityContext: The jewellery store inside Landmark Plaza visited in episode 8, a scene that reflects Sakuta's earnest but financially stretched attempts to give Mai a meaningful gift.

ingredients.name: Yokohama World Porters
ingredients.localName: 横浜ワールドポーターズ

ingredients.localName: 横浜ハンマーヘッド第3駐車場

ingredients.name: Yokohama Red Brick Warehouse
ingredients.entityContext: The landmark red brick buildings along Yokohama's waterfront appear in episode 7's Minato Mirai sequence, framing a key emotional exchange between the leads.

ingredients.name: Zou-no-hana Park
ingredients.entityContext: The curved waterfront park beside the Yokohama Bay where Sakuta and Mai walk together in episode 7, its open sea views and historic pier creating one of the film's most romantic outdoor moments.

ingredients.name: Zou-no-hana Park
ingredients.entityContext: The curved waterfront park beside the Yokohama Bay where Sakuta and Mai walk together in episode 7, its open sea views and historic pier creating one of the film's most romantic outdoor moments.

ingredients.name: Sun Aloha Minato Mirai
ingredients.localName: サンアロハ みなとみらい山下公園本店

ingredients.name: Nihon-Odori Station
ingredients.entityContext: The Minatomirai Line station beneath the broad Nihon-odori boulevard, used in episode 7 as a transit point in the Yokohama waterfront sequence.

ingredients.name: Aobadai Station
ingredients.entityContext: The Tokyu Den-en-toshi Line station in Yokohama's Aoba ward used in episode 7 as Sakuta travels across the city, a background transit location in the film's Kanagawa geography.

ingredients.name: Shichirigahama Station
ingredients.entityContext: The quaint Enoden station overlooking the sea, iconic from the Bunny Girl Senpai series; in this film it appears in episode 7 scenes tied to Shoko's storyline and the Enoden coastal route.

ingredients.name: FamilyMart; Fujisawa Station North Exit
ingredients.localName: ファミリーマート 藤沢駅北口店

ingredients.name: CoCo Ichibanya Fujisawae Station North
ingredients.localName: カレーハウスCoCo壱番屋 藤沢駅北口店

ingredients.name: Yukiai Bridge
ingredients.entityContext: The small bridge near Shichirigahama beach, a recurring Bunny Girl Senpai location that returns in this film for a quiet coastal scene between Sakuta and Shoko.

ingredients.name: Yokohama City University - Kanazawa-Hakkei Campus
ingredients.localName: 横浜市立大学 金沢八景キャンパス
ingredients.entityContext: The university campus that stands in for the fictional Minegahara University, which is central to the entire Rascal series as the school attended by Sakuta and Mai.

ingredients.name: Sakuragicho Station
ingredients.entityContext: The JR station at the gateway to Minato Mirai used as a transit point in episode 4, its distinctive pedestrian ropeway visible nearby.

ingredients.name: MARK IS Minatomirai
ingredients.localName: MARK IS みなとみらい
ingredients.entityContext: The large shopping complex in Yokohama's Minato Mirai visited during episode 4's city-trip scenes, its glass facade and walkways recognisable in the background.

ingredients.localName: ロイヤルパレス七番館

ingredients.name: Fujisawa Station
ingredients.entityContext: The main hub station for Sakuta's daily life, used repeatedly throughout the film as a departure and arrival point that anchors nearly every plot thread.

ingredients.entityContext: The main hub station for Sakuta's daily life, used repeatedly throughout the film as a departure and arrival point that anchors nearly every plot thread.

ingredients.name: Enoshima-ohashi Bridge
ingredients.entityContext: The bridge connecting the Enoshima island to the mainland, crossed by Sakuta in a pivotal episode 4 scene as he rushes toward a key encounter, familiar to fans of the whole Bunny Girl Senpai series.

ingredients.name: Enoshima Koya
ingredients.entityContext: The rustic seafood and snack stall at the Enoshima island entrance area where characters stop during the episode 4 island visit.

ingredients.name: Katase-Enoshima Station
ingredients.entityContext: The ornate Enoshima station building on the Enoden line, a Bunny Girl Senpai series landmark, appears in episode 4 as Sakuta's point of departure for the island.

ingredients.name: Surf Coaster
ingredients.localName: サーフコースター リヴァイアサン
ingredients.entityContext: The distinctive rollercoaster at Hakkeijima Sea Paradise visible in the episode 3 amusement park scenes, its looping track over the water a recognisable pilgrimage landmark.

ingredients.name: Yokohama Hakkeijima Sea Paradise
ingredients.localName: 横浜・八景島シーパラダイス
ingredients.entityContext: The waterfront amusement park where Sakuta and his group visit in episode 3, its colorful oceanside setting forming a backdrop for a bittersweet group scene.

ingredients.name: Hakkeijima Station
ingredients.entityContext: The Seaside Line station serving the Hakkeijima island park, where characters arrive for the episode 3 outing and the sea-view platform underscores the film's coastal mood.

ingredients.name: Hakkeijima Station
ingredients.entityContext: The Seaside Line station serving the Hakkeijima island park, where characters arrive for the episode 3 outing and the sea-view platform underscores the film's coastal mood.

ingredients.name: Anniversaire Cafe

ingredients.entityContext: The waterfront cafe in Yokohama's Minato Mirai where Sakuta and Mai share a quiet romantic moment, the elegant setting contrasting with the emotional turmoil of the plot.

ingredients.name: Odaiba Marine Park Pier
ingredients.entityContext: The Odaiba seaside promenade where characters walk with the Rainbow Bridge and Tokyo skyline behind them, one of the film's most visually striking outdoor settings.

ingredients.name: Unicorn Gundam
ingredients.localName: 実物大ユニコーンガンダム立像
ingredients.entityContext: The life-size Unicorn Gundam statue on Odaiba's DiverCity plaza appears in the background during episode 3's Tokyo excursion, a recognisable landmark fans can photograph at the exact angle.

ingredients.name: Zepp DiverCity (TOKYO)

ingredients.name: Tayasu-mon Gate
ingredients.entityContext: The historic Edo-period gate near Budokan appears in a pivotal scene where the timeline mystery connected to Shoko deepens, giving the urban Tokyo backdrop a melancholy weight.
